title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Browser and Node Client Systems · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for redirect semantics, method rewriting, credential scope, and loop control, producing auditable redirect policy engine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch auditable redirect policy engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Resolve Location references against the current request URL and validate the resulting destination each hop. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Bound redirect hops, detect loops, retain the chain, and expose the policy decision to callers. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Manual Redirect Defense
steps.instruction:
review case — offline design-system client sends POST T-3 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step. Competency probe: Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Inspect the Manual Redirect Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Manual Redirect Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, ssrf, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Manual Redirect Defense, keep Manual Redirect Defense connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: For Manual Redirect Defense, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step. Competency probe: Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. For Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Manual Redirect Defense, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Following redirects is transport plumbing with no application or security semantics. This remains false for TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using choose and automatic plus specific case evidence.
